name: Christmas Tree Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1 and a half packages Softened cream cheese, 8 oz
1 and a half packages Confectioners' sugar, 16 oz
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease a 15 x 10 x 1-inch jelly roll pan; line with waxed paper and grease the paper.
3. Prepare the cake mix according to package directions for a "lite" recipe, mixing in the orange peel.
4. Pour batter into the prepared pan and bake for 20 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted comes out clean.
5. Allow the cake to cool in the pan for at least 15 minutes.
6. Invert the cake onto a board covered with waxed paper; lift it from the pan and remove the paper from the bottom of the cake.
7. Let the cake cool completely.
8. While the cake is cooling, cut triangle patterns from lightweight cardboard with bases of 4 inches, 3 inches, 2.5 inches, and 2.25 inches.
9. Using the cardboard patterns, carefully cut triangles from the cooled cake, fitting the patterns as necessary to use all of the cake.
10. Remove any waxed paper from under the cake.
11. In a large bowl with mixer on medium speed, beat together butter, cream cheese, and vanilla until light and fluffy.
12. Gradually beat in confectioners' sugar; continue beating for 5-7 minutes until thoroughly blended and smooth.
13. Stir about half of the frosting and green food coloring to achieve the desired shade.
14. Spread the frosting over the top and sides of each cake triangle using a flexible metal spatula, smoothing it on or swirling gently to resemble tree branches.
15. Decorate the cakes as desired with candies, colored sugars, assorted sprinkles, and nonpareils.
16. Gently hollow out a small area in the center of the bottom edge of each tree using a tip of a small knife; insert caramel rolls or licorice sticks for trunks.
